title: Optimize home agent session first-paint latency
prompt:
The Kanban home/sidebar agent session takes a noticeable moment to appear every time the page opens. Profile and reduce that first-paint latency.

Background (already traced):
- Each open calls loadTaskChatMessages → backend getTaskChatMessages / loadTaskSessionMessages, which reads the persisted JSONL transcript from disk (~/.kanban or board worktree sessions/<taskId>/messages.jsonl) and mergeSessionMessages(persisted, live). perf16 (commit c7852) memoized the MERGE per task, but the FIRST open still pays the disk read + the tRPC/ws round-trip before anything renders.
- Relevant: web-ui/src/hooks/use-kanban-chat-session.ts (initial onLoadMessages effect), the home panel load path, and the backend session-message journal read.

Step 1 — PROFILE first, don't guess: measure where the wait actually is (backend disk read+parse vs transport vs client render) for a home session with a realistic transcript. Report findings before optimizing.

Then optimize the dominant cost. Candidate directions (pick based on profiling, keep it minimal — no over-engineering):
- Client-side cache the last-rendered transcript per home session and render it INSTANTLY on open, then revalidate/merge fresh data in the background (stale-while-revalidate).
- Warm/prefetch the current home session's messages on connection (before the panel mounts) so the fetch is already in flight / cached.
- Tail-first load: fetch only the most recent N messages for first paint, lazy-load older history on scroll-up.

Constraints:
- Don't break the existing getTaskChatMessages merge/journal contract or the perf16 merge cache.
- Keep the wire contract stable if possible.
- web:typecheck + biome clean; no console.* (use the logger facade).

Acceptance:
- A profiling note identifying the dominant latency source.
- A measurable reduction in time-to-first-message on page open for an existing home session.
- No regression to message correctness, streaming, or restart-history persistence.
